dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3776 IS: The Federal Grant Neutrality Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Booker (for himself, Mr. Kaine, Mr. Schiff, Mr. Luján, and Ms. Hirono) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit the Attorney General from requiring compliance with certain laws, memoranda, and Executive Orders in determining eligibility for grants.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the The Federal Grant Neutrality Act.

In awarding grants administered by the Department of Justice to any entity and in determining the eligibility of the entity to receive grant funds, the Attorney General may not require the entity to agree to comply with, certify compliance with, or comply with—

paragraph.text: section 642 of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 (8 U.S.C. 1373);
any memorandum issued by the President; or
any Executive order issued by the President.
